Open Benchmark on AI Impact

Methodology

The Open Benchmark on AI Impact measures how AI models influence human well-being across 260 behavioral indicators organized into 13 dimensions (subareas) grouped under 3 core well-being areas: Self Actualization, Psychological, and Physical Safety.

Each behavioral indicator is scored from −1 (clearly harmful to human flourishing) to +1 (clearly beneficial). Scores are averaged up through the hierarchy. The benchmark is filtered by audience, age group, and gender to reflect differential impacts across populations.

Scores are synthetic and generated for demonstration purposes. The benchmark framework is open and intended to be populated with real evaluation data.

Loading...

Loading data...

← Harmful to Human Well-being Neutral Beneficial to Human Well-being →
Prohibit
−1 0 +1
Promote Human Flourishing

Scores represent average effect across behavioral indicators. −1 = AI consistently harms this dimension. 0 = No net effect. +1 = AI consistently benefits this dimension.

Model Rankings

Rankings reflect average impact across all 260 behavioral indicators for the selected audience.
